mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not statistically higher than baseline when UNRATE remains above 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust real demand without inflationary pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative when HOUST rises and CPIAUCSL remains flat over a 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average during a post-recession recovery phase signals capacity utilization normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during the first 18 months following a recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and payroll expansion confirm broad-based economic momentum, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as institutional investors increase equity allocations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns when both GDPC1 growth and PAYEMS growth are above their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales and food services revenues reflects weakening aggregate demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite a 3-month consecutive decline in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates coinciding with a rising unemployment rate indicate a restrictive monetary policy stance that is actively cooling the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE rise simultaneously for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, which drags down broader economic sentiment and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when HOUST remains below 1.2 million for two consecutive months.
